commit.message:
fix: defensive alignment check in RtPtrs::is_cached + clippy fixes

- Add bytecode pointer null+alignment check to is_cached() to
  prevent misaligned dereference crash (address 0x200000002) seen
  in debug test suite for JSON.parse reviver tests.
- Fix clippy dead_code warning: allow unused NodeOrImm::Imm variant
  (retained for future stub calls).
- Fix clippy needless_range_loop in CreateObjectLiteralWithProperties
  zero-fill loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Auto-deploy to Azure after successful Test and Lint workflow

Triggers on workflow_run completion of 'Test and Lint' on main,
only when the test workflow succeeded. Manual dispatch still works.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: UI improvements, bug fixes, and performance optimizations

## UI Improvements
- BottomComposer: Refactored layout with fixed prompt prefix ('CODEXA AGENT | ') for consistent branding
- TopHeader: Enhanced header display with improved styling and organization
- Timeline: Improved timeline rendering and event display
- AgentBlock: Updated agent display with cleaner layout
- ThinkingBlock: Enhanced thinking state visualization
- TurnGroup: Improved turn organization and spacing
- Markdown: Refined markdown rendering for better readability

## Performance Optimizations
- BottomComposer: Added React.memo with intelligent comparison function to prevent unnecessary re-renders during streaming responses
- Optimized memoization to only re-render when meaningful state changes (persona, input, display props, layout)
- Streaming updates within RESPONDING state no longer trigger full component re-renders
- Status line updates now independent of component renders

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed status message character encoding issues (ellipsis and special characters)
- Improved input field display when locked (answer mode)
- Fixed locked input visual feedback and status messages
- Corrected status message text: 'waiting for your answer' instead of 'Assistant needs one more answer'

## Infrastructure & Testing
- Updated test files (AgentBlock.test.ts, TopHeader.test.tsx, focusFlow.test.tsx)
- Added new test coverage (inputBuffer.test.ts)
- Added outputPipeline.ts module for output processing
- Enhanced codexTranscript.ts provider functionality
- Improved chatLifecycle.ts session handling

## Files Modified (16 total)
- bin/codexa.js: CLI entry point (53 insertions/deletions)
- src/app.tsx: App state and lifecycle (82 insertions/deletions)
- src/index.tsx: Entry point enhancements (14 insertions/deletions)
- src/core/providers/codexTranscript.ts: Provider improvements (15 insertions)
- src/session/chatLifecycle.ts: Session lifecycle (8 insertions/deletions)
- src/ui/: UI components and tests (multiple files, 175+ insertions/deletions)

## Stats
- 362 insertions(+)
- 270 deletions(-)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

starship: replace literal NerdFont glyphs with unicode escape sequences

Convert all Private Use Area (PUA) glyphs to portable TOML unicode escapes:
- BMP PUA (U+E000–U+F8FF): use \uXXXX
- Supplementary PUA-A (U+F0000–U+FFFFF): use \UXXXXXXXX

Standard Unicode characters (⇡ ⇣ ⇕ ❯ ❮ ✘ » …) are left as literals
since they are not NerdFont-specific.

Also add .gitattributes to enforce UTF-8 / LF for all text files in the repo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(maglev): inline IC fast path for LoadNamedGeneric + fix type error

- Emit inline property IC probe directly in Maglev codegen for
  LoadNamedGeneric, avoiding the full stub call overhead on IC hits.
  Falls back to the generic stub on IC miss.
- Fix type mismatch in deferred array-method IC return (Some(Ok(v))
  should be Some(v) since the function returns Option<i64>).
- Add LoadNamedGeneric to R15 keyed-access detection so the RT_PTRS
  TLS address is cached in R15 when property loads are present.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove em dash from X-Agent-Label HTTP response header

Em dash U+2014 (char value 8212) causes ByteString error in fetch.
HTTP headers only accept ASCII (0-255 range).
Replace — with - in ATLAS carousel response header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix eval completion value and arrow function body for multi-statement sources

- Remove the string-based __fn_body__ re-wrapping heuristic for
  VmFunction/VmClosure values returned by eval. Since merge_eval_chunk
  properly merges bytecode into the host chunk, these values are
  directly callable without re-wrapping. The old heuristic failed for
  multi-statement sources like '{}() => 42;' where tail extraction
  incorrectly included preceding statements.

- Fix empty block completion value propagation: when an empty block
  is the last statement (is_last=true) in a statement list with a
  completion variable, load the saved completion value instead of
  leaving the stack empty. This fixes eval('{42}{}') returning
  undefined instead of 42.

Fixes: eval-block-with-statment-block, eval-block-arrow-function-assignment-expr,
       eval-class-arrow-function-assignment-expr, eval-fn-arrow-function-assignment-expr

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add lambda-react-ui stack and lambda-api csv support (#261)

## Summary
- add a new lambda-react-ui implementation (React 19 + Zustand + Vite 7
on port 3090) with full application tracking UI, interview stages,
history panel, CSV import/export modal, responsive layout, and unit
tests
- extend lambda-api with CSV import/export/template endpoints and cursor
pagination support, including CSV parsing/serialization tests
- wire the new stack into monorepo scripts, Playwright config, e2e
orchestration scripts, docs, launch config, cspell, and feature spec
artifacts under specs/026-lambda-react-ui

## Review fixes (Iterations 1–13)
- **ContextPanel**: salary NaN guard → `isFinite` + `Math.round`,
Notes/SpecialRequirements → `TextArea`, salary/notes blur wrapped in
try/catch
- **csv.service**: enum validation; `toNumber` rejects decimals and
negatives; `offerDueDate`/`isArchived` independent conditions; unused
param removed; eliminate double CSV parse
- **exportApplications**: while-loop pagination; date-stamped
`Content-Disposition`; `includeArchived` defaults to `true`
- **application.service**: `if (cursor !== undefined)`; cursor token
encodes only `{ page }`
- **api.ts**: `URL.revokeObjectURL` deferred via `setTimeout(..., 100)`
- **FilterBar / CsvImportModal**: `<a href="#">` → `<button
type="button">`; rejections caught and surfaced
- **Modal**: body scroll lock added
- **ApplicationForm**: `offerDueDate` fully wired; client-side
validation for salary and skillsMatch
- **ApplicationEditPage**: modal a11y; "Back to List" uses
`navigate("/")`; stages use `Promise.all`; API errors caught; delete
catches errors
- **applicationStore**: stages use `Promise.all`; `restoreToVersion`
sets error and re-throws; `loadSelectedApplication` clears before load
- **HistoryPanel**: removed double-fetch timer; `restoreToVersion`
errors surfaced; close button `aria-label`
- **Sidebar**: removed misleading counts; collapse/expand `aria-label`
- **FilterBar selects**: `aria-label` on all 4 selects
- **TextArea**: `{...props}` before `onMouseUp`
- **utils.ts**: `getTodayDate()` uses local date parts; test fixed for
timezone flakiness
- **ListPage**: removed redundant `loadSelectedApplication` call from
`handleSelect`
- **ApplicationCard**: derive `days` constant to avoid `number|null` in
`Math.abs`
- **InterviewStageForm**: `handleSave` wrapped in try/catch; `onDelete`
catches errors
- **InterviewStageItem**: checkbox `onUpdate` catches errors;
`onConfirm` async with .then/.catch; `itemError` displayed
- **run-e2e.sh**: `ensure_dynamodb_local` added
- **csv-import-export.spec.ts**: selectors updated to `:is(a,
button):has-text(...)`
- **CLAUDE.md / README.md / specs**: version and contract doc
corrections

## Review fixes (Iterations 14–20)
- **FilterBar**: `<a href="#">` links for Export CSV and Template
replaced with `<button type="button">`; `csvError` state surfaces
download failures
- **applicationStore**: `deleteApplication`, `archiveApplication`,
`restoreApplication` wrapped in try/catch, set store `error`, rethrow
- **applicationStore**: cursor pagination branch uses synthetic total
(`hasMore ? page*limit+1 : (page-1)*limit+items.length`) instead of
`response.items.length`
- **ApplicationList**: `handleConfirm` catch block added; `useEffect`
auto-pages back to last valid page when current page exceeds total after
delete/archive; `gridTemplateColumns` responds to `viewMode` prop
- **ApplicationEditPage**: `deleteApplication` →
`navigateAfterSave('/')` so `isDirty` is cleared and `useBlocker`
doesn't intercept navigation
- **InterviewStageForm / InterviewStageItem**: unhandled promise
rejections on save/delete/update/confirm — all surfaced via error state
- **ContextPanel**: close button `aria-label="Close context panel"`;
salary blur guard extended to reject negatives
- **vite.config.ts**: rewrite callback param renamed from `path` to
`requestPath` to avoid shadowing the imported `path` module
- **.dockerignore**: removed `Dockerfile*` line that was excluding
Dockerfiles from build context
- **run-e2e.sh**: `load_env_file()` helper; `ensure_dynamodb_local` uses
`npm --prefix "$ROOT_DIR" run migrate:lambda-api` and `docker compose
--project-directory "$ROOT_DIR"`
- **lambda-api unit tests**: cursor pagination encode/decode tests added
(cursor='start', valid token, malformed token fallback, last-page
nextCursor=null, has-more nextCursor encoding, round-trip invariant)

## Security fixes
- **hono-api, nest-api, nuxt-api**: upgrade `drizzle-orm` to `0.45.2`
(exact) to fix GHSA-gpj5-g38j-94v9 (SQL injection via improperly escaped
SQL identifiers, high severity)

## Test plan
- [ ] `npm run build:lambda-react-ui` and `npm run build:lambda-api`
pass
- [ ] `npm run lint:lambda-react-ui` and `npm run lint:lambda-api` pass
- [ ] `npm run test:lambda-api` passes (unit + CSV + cursor pagination
tests)
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e:lambda-react-ui` passes (requires DynamoDB Local
on port 8000)

commit.message:
fix: move action_result inside state to match Yandex Smart Home API

Per Yandex Smart Home API spec and dext0r reference implementation,
action_result must be nested inside the 'state' object alongside
'instance' and 'value', not as a sibling of 'state'.

Before: {type, state: {instance, value}, action_result: {status}}
After:  {type, state: {instance, value, action_result: {status}}}

Introduced CapabilityActionResultState dataclass to hold the correct
structure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add external tradingview research references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): LEA for CheckedSmiMultiply by 3/5/9

When the left operand is provably i32-range, replace IMUL+JO with LEA:
  LEA dst, [src + src*scale]  (scale=2 for ×3, 4 for ×5, 8 for ×9)
Since i32 × 9 < i64::MAX, 64-bit overflow is impossible and the JO
deopt check can be elided entirely.  Saves 2 instructions per multiply
(IMUL→LEA + skip JO) in arithmetic-heavy loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: albacore gnome50 libinput + rechunk disk pressure

- build_scripts/gnome.sh: enable jreilly1821/c10s-gnome-50 alongside
  c10s-gnome-50-fresh for gnome50 EL10 builds. libinput 1.31.1 was
  published to c10s-gnome-50 (not c10s-gnome-50-fresh), so mutter's
  libinput >= 1.27.0 requirement was unsatisfied on albacore.

- Justfile: move 'rm -f out.ociarchive' to immediately after podman load
  instead of after podman build. Holding the OCI archive on disk while
  the loaded image and final build image both exist causes disk pressure
  that triggers the podman storage index corruption bug (image not known).
  Also add an explicit null-check on LOADED_ID for a clearer error message
  if the load fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: architecture smell fixes (8 issues)

- websocket_server: replace PackedByteArray client_id with int throughout;
  remove redundant wrapping/unwrapping; simplify send_to_player debug noise
- game_manager/message_handler: remove client_id from players dict; add
  _player_to_client map to MessageHandler so network concerns stay in network layer
- game_board_3d: emit game_over_triggered signal instead of directly calling
  game_over_panel.show_result(); remove game_over_panel reference from board
- game_over_panel: self-hide on game_started signal (no longer driven by board)
- main.gd: wire game_over_triggered -> show_result; remove dead game_over_panel assign
- message_handler: rename _broadcast_round_ready -> on_round_display_complete;
  remove 4 never-emitted signals (handle_join/turn_submit/ready/leave)
- card_validator: add null/empty guard for hand_instance_ids
- card_base/registry/sprint/orchestrator/deck_config: rename TYPE_RUSH -> TYPE_SPRINT
- turn_manager: document turn_executed event dict schema in ## comment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): defer array-method IC + LICM DeleteProperty safety

- Defer RT_ARRAY_METHOD_IC TLS lookup after property IC hit path,
  saving ~5-10ns on every own-property or prototype IC hit
- Add DeleteProperty to LICM mutated-objects and side-effects lists
  for correctness (prevents hoisting loads past property deletions)

commit.message:
EXP-1171-1180: Causal benchmark — LSTM overfits, Enhanced XGBoost CV=0.477

Key discoveries:
- LSTM pipeline HURTS in 5-fold CV (-0.068) — was overfitting single split
- Enhanced features are TRUE BEST: +0.023 in CV, all 11 patients improve
- XGBoost hyperparameter tuning: +0.026 (11/11 wins)
- Multi-horizon regularization: +0.013 (11/11 wins)
- Residual autocorrelation=0.474 — temporal structure exists but LSTM can't capture it
- Raw glucose encoding is optimal over delta/relative/quantile
- Per-patient optimal depth varies: d3 (small) to d8 (complex)
- Revised SOTA: R²=0.477 (5-fold CV) vs previous R²=0.581 (overfitted single split)

Campaign total: 180 experiments (EXP-1001-1180), validated Δ=+0.123 over naive baseline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update src/static/app.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ai): add Cohere direct provider + dynamic OpenRouter free model discovery

- Add Cohere as 11th provider with OpenAI-compatible endpoint
  (https://api.cohere.ai/compatibility/v1/chat/completions)
  Models: command-a-03-2025, command-r-plus-08-2024, command-r-08-2024
  Free trial tier: 1000 calls/month, 20 req/min

- Add discoverOpenRouterFreeModels() — auto-discovers new free models
  from OpenRouter API at runtime, appends to chain, pre-exhausts stale
  models. Falls back to static list if API unreachable.

- Update static OpenRouter list: remove 11 stale models no longer free
  (Mistral Small 3.1/3.2, DeepSeek R1/R1-Zero/V3, Phi-4, Kimi K2,
  Qwen 2.5 72B, Mistral Nemo, Llama 4 Maverick)

- Add 6 new OpenRouter free models: Gemma 4 31B, Gemma 4 26B MoE,
  GPT-OSS 20B, Nemotron 12B VL, Dolphin Mistral 24B, Gemma 3n E4B

- Add COHERE_API_KEY to load-rc-env.mjs mapping
- Bump to ai-models v13 (80+ models, 11 providers)

commit.message:
docs(readme): document targeting changes from last three commits

Updates cover:
- Default-workspace message is blue 'Info:' (not yellow warning)
- All targeting errors (no match, flag conflicts) print in red
- --all is mutually exclusive with --project-name and --repo-name;
  all flag constraints listed in a dedicated bullet list
- --repo-name is a substring filter (not exact/segment match);
  may match multiple repos; yellow warning shown when it does;
  red error when it matches nothing
- Command examples updated to use -p/-r shorthands and show partial
  match semantics (e.g. '-r api' / '-r service' instead of exact names)
- Common flags table: --repo-name description updated to say 'substring filter'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
